Welcome to the Open Water Course!

Please respond to this event only after you have been in touch with us and got a spot confirmation. 


Please use chat within this event for questions and organizational matters.

Students, please make sure to subscribe to notifications on this event and the one created for the following training weekend to stay updated!

Students have to be double vaxed

Course outline (subject to change depending on the weather or other reasons)

Orientation date will commence near starting period

Enjoy your training!

——
**Gear Hire Policy**
Please bring a valid Photo-ID (to be deposited for the hire duration) to hire gear and ensure you have already paid the one-off gear hire deposit. 

**—COVID Safety—** 
- You must be fully vaccinated to attend or work the event unless you have a valid medical exemption. 
- Complete a symptom self-assessment prior to leaving home. Do not come to the event if you are unwell, even with very mild symptoms
- Do not attend if you are feeling unwell, or experience any of the following symptoms: 
**Runny nose / cough / aches / pain / nausea / vomiting / diarrhoea / sore throat / loss of smell or taste / fever / chills / nigh sweats / headache / difficulty breathing**
- Maintain 1.5 metres distance from others during the event, where possible.
- Do not come to the event if you are required to isolate or quarantine for any reason and/or you are awaiting results of a COVID-19 test.
- Minimise movement as much as possible by staying within your allotted space or seat.
- Follow directions for face coverings and hygiene during the event.
- Our COVID-19 safety officer is Burak, you must notify him (or any available committee member) if you feel unwell after the trip, test positive, or have any other queries related to COVID-19 safety protocols. 
- Please refer to University Coronavirus Website for more information https://www.unimelb.edu.au/coronavirus as well as MUSport Guidelines: https://sport.unimelb.edu.au/play-sport/club-resources/club-return-to-sport

Trip Leader: Andrea Ceriani - Andrew Hebbard
Leader Phone: 0401 224 088
Secondary Trip Contact: Andrea Ceriani 0449714402
Trip Type: Boat 
Difficulty: OW with boat diving experience 
No. of Planned Dives: 2pp
Mandatory Equipment: DSMB, catch bag if you think you might like a cray
Please note that the event start time is the meeting time at the shed unless stated otherwise in the briefing

—Briefing— 
Water Temperature: 14
Weather Conditions: 26 degrees, sunny

Description
Lets get back in the water for some diving this Thursday! We will be planning on diving some rare dive sites around cape schanck and flinders back beach 

—COVID Safety— 
- All participants must show Covid vaccine certificate (2Xdoses) prior to participation as per Melbourne University
- Do not attend if you are feeling unwell, or experience any of the following symptoms: 
Runny nose / cough / aches / pain / nausea / vomiting / diarrhoea / sore throat / loss of smell or taste / fever / chills / nigh sweats / headache / difficulty breathing
- Please familiarise yourself with hygiene protocols present in the club and use hand sanitisers
- Our COVID-19 safety officer is Burak, you must notify him (or any available committee member) if you feel unwell after the trip, test positive, or have any other queries related to COVID-19 safety protocols. 
- Please refer to the “Educating your members” section of this link for current UoM Covid safety procedures and expectations. https://sport.unimelb.edu.au/play-sport/club-resources/club-return-to-sport

**Description**
It’s finally time to dive Lorne pier! Reported as a nice dive destination, the place has gained some reputation for alleged mako and white shark sightings. 

Miraculous by my standards, but it should do for a great atypical dive nonetheless! 

This trip will occur one day of Feb weekends, we will confirm specific weekend as weather becomes apparent.

Welcome to the new term year with students returning to campus! Followed by the orientation day, we will be hosting a BBQ night at the shed for more info sessions and drinks. BBQ cook-up will begin at around 6 till late. 

If you are interested in joining the club and will like to know more, including club membership, courses, gear hires, club trips and more. This will be a great chance to meet most of the committees which you will see very often once you join the club.

The BBQ has a contribution fee of $5, this can be paid through team app or cash.

—COVID Safety—
- You must be fully vaccinated to attend or work the event unless you have a valid medical exemption.
- Complete a symptom self-assessment prior to leaving home. Do not come to the event if you are unwell, even with very mild symptoms
- Do not attend if you are feeling unwell, or experience any of the following symptoms:
Runny nose / cough / aches / pain / nausea / vomiting / diarrhoea / sore throat / loss of smell or taste / fever / chills / nigh sweats / headache / difficulty breathing
- Maintain 1.5 metres distance from others during the event, where possible.
- Do not come to the event if you are required to isolate or quarantine for any reason and/or you are awaiting results of a COVID-19 test.
- Minimise movement as much as possible by staying within your allotted space or seat.
- Follow directions for face coverings and hygiene during the event.
- Our COVID-19 safety officer is Burak, you must notify him (or any available committee member) if you feel unwell after the trip, test positive, or have any other queries related to COVID-19 safety protocols.
- Please refer to University Coronavirus Website for more information https://www.unimelb.edu.au/coronavirus as well as MUSport Guidelines: https://sport.unimelb.edu.au/play-sport/club-resources/club-return-to-sport

Introduction
This course is designed to train divers in the use of a diver propulsion
vehicle (DPV) and familiarizes them with the skills, knowledge, planning,
organization, procedures, techniques, problems, and hazards of using a DPV
in a non-overhead environment.
Student Prerequisites
1. SDI Open Water Scuba Diver or equivalent
2. Minimum age 18, 15 with parental consent

rental DPV available

the course is conducted in full 1-day session 

minimum 4 students to run the course

Thursday orientation night (the official first day of the course) on 05/05/2022

(Once insurance is renewed, please set a new reminder for the following year). 

2005 TOYOTA LANDCRUISER GXL (4X4) UZJ100R 4D
WAGO 5 SP AUTOMATIC, V8, 4.7L
Registration 1MK9TI

Amount Covered:
$19,600
Period of Insurance:
18 February 2022 to 11:59pm 18 February 2023
Legal Liability:
$20 million
Address where car is kept overnight
UNI SPORT 103 TIN ALLEY,
PARKVILLE VIC 3052

the course is conducted in full 1-day session 

minimum 4 students to run the course

event_name: Dive Photo Sharing - My Best Diving Moments
description:
Thank you all for uploading pictures to the gallery and tagging us on all socials, we love sharing diving moments, happy faces during surface intervals, bbq after dives, fascinating marine life, and mastering an underwater skill... the diving journey with friends is just as enjoyable as the dives itself! 

How to enter the contest:
Send us a picture (Facebook, Instagram, in person, gallery uploads)
Must include: Your name and a caption for the picture

The TDI Intro to Tech course introduces students to the world of technical diving. This course is designed as an introductory course to the TDI Advanced Nitrox & Decompression Procedures Course. The objective of this course is to familiarize students with technical equipment configurations, to enhance open water diving skills (such as buoyancy, trim, and situational awareness), and to introduce students to advanced gas planning techniques within a no-decompression context. This course is strictly a no-decompression course; students are permitted to use enriched air nitrox (EAN) mixes, provided the gas mix is within their current level of certification. Intro to Tech may be combined with TDI Advanced Nitrox at the discretion of the instructor.

Pre-requisites:
Minimum age 18, 15 with parental consent
Minimum certification an SDI Open Water Scuba Diver or equivalent
Provide proof of 25 logged open water dives

Course outline (subject to change depending on the weather or other reasons)

minimum 2 students to run the course

Thursday orientation night at shed (the official first day of the course) on 26/05/2022

This course provides the training required to allow candidates to competently and safely blend nitrox gas. The objective of this course is to train candidates in the proper techniques, equipment requirements and hazards involved in blending nitrox gases for recreational scuba. Upon successful completion of this course, graduates may engage in the blending of nitrox gases, without direct supervision.

Pre-requisites:
Minimum age 18

minimum 4 students to run the course

event_name: SA mt gambier  Fresh water diving trip
description:
We are planning a trip to mt gambier fresh water diving 

One of the best diving experiences you can have in Australia 

do not miss it out!!!! 

Itinerary -draft- subject to change due to conditions of trip

Departure to SA at 2 pm from the shed on 1st of July (Friday) 

diving at ewends pond , kilsby sinkhole  2nd of July (Saturday) 

Morning Snorkel at PICCANINNIE PONDS  3rd of July (SUNDAY)  



After lunch Returning Melbourne – planning to arrive shed before dinner time! 

Trip costs (confirmed) – $500 (100 deposited already) 


What's includes 

2 nights at dive lodge 

Diving & snorkel fees for 

ewends pond , kilsby sinkhole, PICCANINNIE PONDS 
Agency fee. Rental gear. Air fillings 

NOT INCLUDES 
oil share fee ( 1000kms returned trip) 

Food

event_name: TDI Advanced Nitrox & Decompression Procedures Diver
description:
This course examines the theory, methods, and procedures of planned stage decompression diving. This program is designed to combine the TDI Advanced Nitrox and TDI Decompression Procedures course into one course. The objective of this course is to train divers how to plan and conduct a standard staged decompression dive not exceeding a maximum depth of 45 meters unless taught in conjunction with the TDI Extended Range Course. The most common equipment requirements, equipment set-up, and decompression techniques are presented. Students are permitted to utilize enriched air nitrox (EAN) mixes or oxygen for decompression provided the gas mix is within their current certification level.  Advanced Nitrox is also a great course for those wanting to extend their bottom times in shallower depths such as scientific divers, and a must for SCR or CCR divers.

Pre-requisites:
Have a minimum of Deep Diver Certification.
Have a minimum TDI Nitrox, PADI Enriched Air, or Equivalent.
Show proof of 25 logged open water dives.

Course outline (subject to change depending on the weather or other reasons)

minimum 2 students to run the course

Thursday orientation night at the shed (the official first day of the course) on 30/06/2022

If you have always wanted to try cave diving, experience unparalleled adventures, and see the world beneath the ground, this is where it starts.

Get your entry-level cave certification - CDAA Basic Cave Diver.

A Cavern is a body of freshwater which has a maximum depth of 20 meters, has slight overhangs, near-vertical ascents, and where daylight is always visible (e.g. limestone caverns, dams, flooded quarries, lakes, etc.). A Sinkhole is a body of freshwater which exceeds a depth of 20 meters, can have large passages, and where no restriction is too small for two divers to swim adjacent to each other.

To enroll you must hold an Advanced Open Water recreational SCUBA diver award or equivalent and be qualified as an Open Water diver for a minimum of 12 months. 

Course outline (subject to change depending on the weather or other reasons)

minimum 4 students to run the course

Thursday orientation night at shed (the official first day of the course) on 21/07/2022

event_name: TDI Intro to Tech Diver cancelled
description:
The TDI Intro to Tech course introduces students to the world of technical diving. This course is designed as an introductory course to the TDI Advanced Nitrox & Decompression Procedures Course. The objective of this course is to familiarize students with technical equipment configurations, to enhance open water diving skills (such as buoyancy, trim, and situational awareness), and to introduce students to advanced gas planning techniques within a no-decompression context. This course is strictly a no-decompression course; students are permitted to use enriched air nitrox (EAN) mixes, provided the gas mix is within their current level of certification. Intro to Tech may be combined with TDI Advanced Nitrox at the discretion of the instructor.

Pre-requisites:
Minimum age 18, 15 with parental consent
Minimum certification an SDI Open Water Scuba Diver or equivalent
Provide proof of 25 logged open water dives

Course outline (subject to change depending on the weather or other reasons)

minimum 2 students to run the course

Thursday orientation night at shed (the official first day of the course) on 11/08/2022

Dear Divers!

We are organising a Special General Meeting two recruit two new members. 

We have been having a great year and we have been quite active with diving, BBQs, and refresher courses. Unfortunately, our dear Babak and Kitty are leaving Melbourne. We value everyone's participation, but if you would like to dedicate more time and help us, please raise your hands. 

Location: Melbourne University Dive Shed
Time: 1 September 2022, 6:00 pm



Nominations
Nominations will be held similar to annual meeting, so all nominations must be delivered to the Secretary (Burak) no less than 7 days before the date fixed for the holding of the special general meeting. Cut off is 18:00 AEST 25 August 2022. 
Made in writing (email, whatsaspp, or paper), signed by two members of the Club and accompanied by the written consent of the candidate (which may be endorsed on the form of nomination).
A candidate may only be nominated for one of the two available positions, or as an ordinary member of the committee, prior to the special general meeting.
If insufficient nominations are received to fill all vacancies on the committee, the candidates nominated shall be deemed to be elected and further nominations may be received at the annual general meeting.
If the number of nominations received is equal to the number of vacancies to be filled, the persons nominated shall be deemed to be elected.
If the number of nominations exceeds the number of vacancies to be filled, a ballot must be held.

Committee Roles
( a) a Gear Officer;
( b) an Social Committee Officer,

SGM Agenda
( a) Nominating a new gear officer; and
( b) nominating a new social committee officer;

Once the agenda is completed the regular committee meeting may proceed. 

Burak Uslu
secretary@muuc.org.au
burak.uslu@gmail.com

If you get sea sick easily, please grab some pills. We won’t be able to shelter anywhere during this trip till we are done. Bring lunch too! 

Itinerary: 
- 530 meet at the shed 
- 600 departure 
- 730 arrival at Newhaven Boatramp 
- 800 on the water 
- 830 first dive: The Pinnacles, 40m Tec (Maciej + Liam) 
- 1000 second dive: George Kermode (OW 18m) 
- 1100 Lunch 
- 1200 third dive: Rear Pyramid (OW 18m) 
- 1400 back on shore 
- 1700 back at the shed 
- 1800 finished

Rescue Diver course as the most challenging, yet most rewarding course they’ve ever taken.

​
Why? Because you learn to prevent and manage problems in the water, and become more confident in your skills as a diver, knowing that you can help others if needed.

​
During the course, you learn to become a better buddy by practicing problem solving skills until they become second nature.

event_name: TDI Advanced Nitrox & Decompression Procedures course (ANDP) 4,5,11,12th Feb
description:
TDI Advanced Nitrox course & TDI Decompression Procedures course (ANDP)

Combine your TDI Advanced Nitrox course with the TDI Decompression Procedures course. This will prepare you for planned staged decompression diving and is your first step beyond the normal recreational limits.

The TDI Advanced Nitrox Course qualifies divers to use enriched air nitrox from EAN 21 through EAN 100 percent within your current certification level to a maximum depth of 40 metres/130 feet during dives that do not require staged decompression.

The TDI Decompression Procedures Course prepares you for planned staged decompression diving. With a maximum operating depth of 45 metres/150 feet, this course is your first step beyond the normal sport diving limits. The TDI Decompression Procedures Course combined with the TDI Advanced Nitrox course form the foundation of all other technical courses. After these two courses and some additional experience, the stage has been set for you to move onto additional technical levels.

Course prerequisites:

Minimum age 18

Minimum certification of TDI nitrox diver,PADI enriched diver or equivalent

Proof of 25 logged open water dives

Course outline (subject to change depends on the weather or other reasons)

Orientation date will commence near starting period

any questions to Info.gooddivers@gmail.com 

Cheers

Hi Team
it is just upcoming course notice for someone who may want to do the Nitrox course

*  Use nitrox breathing gas with an oxygen percentage ranging from 22 percent to 40 percent.
*  Plan dives within modified No Decompression units using a programable personal nitrox dive 
    computer or by using dive planning software.
*  Describe the hazards of handling high-oxygen gas mixtures, and the basic requirement for 
    cleaning scuba gear for high-oxygen environments (i.e. mixes up to 40% oxygen)
*  Calculate the Maximum Operating Depth of an analysed nitrox breathing gas at a target depth 
    between 6 meters/20 feet and 40 meters/130 feet and a partial pressure of oxygen between 
    1.0 and 1.6 bar.
*  Understand the relationship between oxygen partial pressure and NOAA time limits to 
    determine oxygen CNS loading.
*   List the signs and symptoms of Central Nervous System oxygen toxicity.
*   Understand how to calibrate a simple oxygen analyser and use it to measure O2 percentage in 
     a cylinder and to familiarize themselves with the standard procedures for gas analysis.
*  Demonstrate how to label an analysed diving cylinder properly.

Let me know if anyone interested
if anyone who wants during weekdays let me know too
info.gooddivers@gmail.com

Alex

event_name: Spider crab research talk 🦀🦀🦀🦀 by guest speaker Elodie
description: After our regular 7pm shed meeting :) we’ll move to Elisabeth Murdoch for the talk (in G06, ground floor entry via glass doors on the side.)\n\nWe know little about the biology and ecology of many marine species across Victoria and the Great Southern Reef. Great spider crabs are a great example. They gather in huge numbers in Port Phillip Bay to moult in winter (and at other times of year for unknown reasons). This phenomenon fascinates the community and has been featured in the media worldwide; yet, we know very little about spider crabs and their gatherings, including their timing, location, durations and where the crabs come from and go afterwards. Elodie is part of a team at Deakin University, which harnesses the power of citizen science in combination with traditional science to find out more about spider crabs, their movements and their ecological role within Victorian waters. She will provide an update on the research that has been carried out to date.

**Trip Info**
Hello, diving enthusiasts!

We are excited to announce that we will be organizing a scuba diving trip to Mount Gambier for the second year in a row! Prepare yourself for an unforgettable underwater adventure in the heart of freshwater diving, including the famous Kilsby Sinkhole. Mark your calendars for the end of July, specifically on the 29th and 30th.

To secure your spot on this incredible scuba diving adventure, we kindly request a deposit of $100. (There will be no refunds for this unforunately) You can make this payment by visiting the "Mt Gambier Trip 2023 DEPOSIT" section in our shop. Once the final number of participants is confirmed, the remaining balance will be posted in the shop for payment.

The total trip cost will cover both accommodation and the guided tour. However, please note that expenses related to food and petrol are not included in the trip cost. We recommend bringing extra cash to cover these personal expenses during the trip.

Gear hire availability will depend on the number of people joining the trip. We are exploring the option of hiring equipment from the MUUC and possibly the tour group during that weekend. We will keep you updated on the gear hire arrangements as we finalize the numbers.

Additionally, we kindly request volunteers who are available and willing to drive during the trip. Your assistance as drivers would be greatly appreciated.

Please also mention you're level of qualification and number of dives :) 

Stay tuned for further updates and details regarding the trip. If you have any questions or need more information, feel free to reach out to our team. We're here to ensure you have a fantastic diving experience!

SDI COMPUTER NITROX

Nitrox is a breathing gas that has a lot of benefits for both new and experienced divers. This course will cover the use of nitrox mixtures from 22 to 40 percent oxygen.

Who this course is for:
The certified diver looking to advance their knowledge and skill set
The certified diver looking to extend their no decompression time limits

Course prerequisites:
Minimum age 18, 10 with parental consent
SDI Open Water Scuba Diver, SDI Junior Open Water Scuba Diver, or equivalent, or current enrollment in one of those courses

What you can expect to learn:
Physiology of using different breathing gas mixtures and equipment considerations for using nitrox
How to safely use nitrox mixtures up to 40 percent with a dive computer
Advantages and disadvantages of utilizing nitrox mixtures, how to analyze a nitrox mixture

What’s in it for you?
The benefits of using Nitrox including:

Increased safety margins
Reduced post dive fatigue
Extended no-decompression times

minimum of 3 students will run
RSVP required

event_name: Boat driver licence course - become a boat driver!
description:
We are organising a boat drivers licence course for our members .

After this course you’ll be able to receive a boat drivers licence at vic roads.

The special price for this course is 145$

+ vicroads licence fees (payable at VicRoads)

This course takes you through everything you need to know to get your Boat and PWC Licence without any pre study.

At the completion of the theory section the there will be an exam for your Boat and PWC endorsement( 16 yrs and older ) and then a Certificate will be issued.

After you just need to present the certificate at Vic Roads to get your Licence. 

Please note you will need to provide an Australian Drivers Licence or Learner Permit or if none is held an Australian Birth Certificate or Passport and Family Medicare Card. 

Books and pens will be provided.


Please note that a minimum of 8 participants are required to run the course.

event_name:
TDI Advanced Nitrox course & TDI Decompression Procedures course (ANDP)
30th Sep, 1st, 7th and 8th Oct
description:
TDI Advanced Nitrox course & TDI Decompression Procedures course (ANDP)

Combine your TDI Advanced Nitrox course with the TDI Decompression Procedures course. This will prepare you for planned staged decompression diving and is your first step beyond the normal recreational limits.

The TDI Advanced Nitrox Course qualifies divers to use enriched air nitrox from EAN 21 through EAN 100 percent within your current certification level to a maximum depth of 40 metres/130 feet during dives that do not require staged decompression.

The TDI Decompression Procedures Course prepares you for planned staged decompression diving. With a maximum operating depth of 45 metres/150 feet, this course is your first step beyond the normal sport diving limits. The TDI Decompression Procedures Course combined with the TDI Advanced Nitrox course form the foundation of all other technical courses. After these two courses and some additional experience, the stage has been set for you to move onto additional technical levels.

Course prerequisites:

Minimum age 18

Minimum certification of TDI nitrox diver,PADI enriched diver or equivalent

Proof of 25 logged open water dives

Course outline (subject to change depends on the weather or other reasons)

Orientation date will commence near starting period

any questions to Info.gooddivers@gmail.com

Cheers

event_name: Guest Speaker Ian Scholey: Diving the Silent Anzac
description:
The AE2 was a submarine of the Australian Navy that was sunk during the Gallipoli campaign of World War I. It now sits at a depth of 72m. 

Guest speaker Ian Scholey is coming to talk to us about his dive at this historically significant wartime wreck, once described as "an underwater time capsule that has not been opened since 1915".

Rescue Diver course as the most challenging, yet most rewarding course they’ve ever taken.

​
Why? Because you learn to prevent and manage problems in the water, and become more confident in your skills as a diver, knowing that you can help others if needed.

​
During the course, you learn to become a better buddy by practicing problem solving skills until they become second nature.

Prerequisites: Adventure Diver/Junior Adventure Diver (or qualifying certification) with completed Underwater Navigation Dive; EFR Primary and Secondary Care training (or qualifying training) within 24 months

event_name: SDI Deep Diver course & Wreck diver course
description:
SDI DEEP DIVER COURSE

SDI’s Deep Diver Course is a must for any diver wishing to explore a little more, go a little deeper, and learn the risks and benefits associated with deep diving. Many interesting sights lay just beyond open water diver training limits such as: beautiful wall dives, shipwrecks, and critters that don’t come into the shallows.The purpose of this course is to provide the necessary training to plan and execute dives that are beyond the depth range experienced during an SDI Open Water Scuba Diver course, specifically beyond 18 metres/60 feet and to a maximum depth of not greater than 40 metres/130 feet. By the end of the course your knowledge, awareness, and comfort level with deep diving activities will have increased.

SDI WRECK DIVER

Wreck diving can be one of the most exciting aspects of sport diving, however every effort must be made to maximize safe diving techniques. The SDI Wreck Diver Course will discuss the equipment and techniques commonly employed while wreck diving. This course may be taught as a non-penetration, 2 dives required, or as a limited-penetration course, requiring 3 dives. Limited-penetration is defined as a swim through or within the ambient light of entry point.




info.gooddivers@gmail.com

Hi Team
it is just upcoming course notice for someone who may want to do the Nitrox course

	Use nitrox breathing gas with an oxygen percentage ranging from 22 percent to 40 percent. 	Plan dives within modified No Decompression units using a programable personal nitrox dive 
computer or by using dive planning software. 	Describe the hazards of handling high-oxygen gas mixtures, and the basic requirement for 
cleaning scuba gear for high-oxygen environments (i.e. mixes up to 40% oxygen) 	Calculate the Maximum Operating Depth of an analysed nitrox breathing gas at a target depth 
between 6 meters/20 feet and 40 meters/130 feet and a partial pressure of oxygen between 
1.0 and 1.6 bar. 	Understand the relationship between oxygen partial pressure and NOAA time limits to 
determine oxygen CNS loading. 	List the signs and symptoms of Central Nervous System oxygen toxicity. 	Understand how to calibrate a simple oxygen analyser and use it to measure O2 percentage in 
a cylinder and to familiarize themselves with the standard procedures for gas analysis. 	Demonstrate how to label an analysed diving cylinder properly. Let me know if anyone interested
if anyone who wants during weekdays let me know too
info.gooddivers@gmail.com

Alex

TDI NITROX GAS BLENDER

Have you ever wondered how your nitrox cylinders are filled? Do you like the “behind the scenes” look at what makes a dive happen? Take the TDI Nitrox Gas Blender Course to find out how your cylinders get filled with nitrox! The TDI Nitrox Gas Blender Course will take you through the blending of nitrox in an easy-to-understand format.This course provides the training required to competently blend nitrox gas. The objective of this course is to train candidates in the proper techniques, equipment requirements and hazards involved in blending nitrox gases for recreational scuba diving activities.

Grab your eggs and get your bunny ears ready, we're making use of the latter half of the mid sem break to run another overnight boating trip wherever the weather is best on the Victorian coastline! Our most likely options are Wilson's Prom, Barwon Heads, Phillip Island or if exceptionally calm we might go Western Vic/Twelve Apostles. 

To secure your spot on this incredible scuba diving adventure, we kindly request a deposit of $100 (There will be no refunds for this unforunately). This will allow us to book the accommodation. We made the course a week earlier.


Use nitrox breathing gas with an oxygen percentage ranging from 22 percent to 40 percent. 	Plan dives within modified No Decompression units using a programable personal nitrox dive 
computer or by using dive planning software. 	Describe the hazards of handling high-oxygen gas mixtures, and the basic requirement for 
cleaning scuba gear for high-oxygen environments (i.e. mixes up to 40% oxygen) 	Calculate the Maximum Operating Depth of an analysed nitrox breathing gas at a target depth 
between 6 meters/20 feet and 40 meters/130 feet and a partial pressure of oxygen between 
1.0 and 1.6 bar. 	Understand the relationship between oxygen partial pressure and NOAA time limits to 
determine oxygen CNS loading. 	List the signs and symptoms of Central Nervous System oxygen toxicity. 	Understand how to calibrate a simple oxygen analyser and use it to measure O2 percentage in 
a cylinder and to familiarize themselves with the standard procedures for gas analysis. 	Demonstrate how to label an analysed diving cylinder properly. Let me know if anyone interested
if anyone who wants during weekdays let me know too
info.gooddivers@gmail.com

Alex

event_name: Gooddivers - TDI Advanced Nitrox & Decompression procedure Course (ANDP)
description:
TDI Advanced Nitrox course & TDI Decompression Procedures course (ANDP)

Combine your TDI Advanced Nitrox course with the TDI Decompression Procedures course. This will prepare you for planned staged decompression diving and is your first step beyond the normal recreational limits.

The TDI Advanced Nitrox Course qualifies divers to use enriched air nitrox from EAN 21 through EAN 100 percent within your current certification level to a maximum depth of 40 metres/130 feet during dives that do not require staged decompression.

The TDI Decompression Procedures Course prepares you for planned staged decompression diving. With a maximum operating depth of 45 metres/150 feet, this course is your first step beyond the normal sport diving limits. The TDI Decompression Procedures Course combined with the TDI Advanced Nitrox course form the foundation of all other technical courses. After these two courses and some additional experience, the stage has been set for you to move onto additional technical levels.

Course prerequisites:

Minimum age 18

Minimum certification of TDI nitrox diver,PADI enriched diver or equivalent

Proof of 25 logged open water dives

Course outline (subject to change depends on the weather or other reasons)

Orientation date will commence near starting period

any questions to Info.gooddivers@gmail.com

Cheers

description:
——
**Trip Info**
——
To celebrate the long weekend I'm putting on a catch-and-cook weekend at my house in Mt. Eliza - At the top of the Mornington Peninsula.

The fun stuff
We will plan for a dive menu consisting of cray-bashing and wreck-diving, and will most likely shuck a scallop drift in the mix as well.

I have room to host 6 people, and hopefully, we will be feasting on well-caught Crayfish and Scallops, while enjoying a beverage and a good time in the evenings after each big day out on the water.

Practicalities:
Don't forget to bring a towel and other essentials for a 2-3 nights stay.
 
If you don't have a current fishing license, you will need to get one(App based, 10$ for the weekend or 39$ for a full year) 

I'll provide coffee and a small keg of beer for the weekend, we will share expenses for food and cook together.

There is also a Woolworths, liquor stores and a few restaurants 10 minutes walking from my house.

Please be aware that I have a small dog (Instagram: Simbalaurier) Who will be expecting belly rubs and kisses, so if you are allergic to dogs, consider yourself warned.

Expected diving location is around the heads and inside the bay, tbc with weather once closer to the date. Diving Sat, Sun and possibly Monday. Mixture of Boat and shore, all weather dependant.

event_name: [Multiweek Boat Trip] [AOW] MUUC explores Northern NSW Week A- CANCELLED
description:
——
**Trip Info**
Meet at shed at 7am!

Greetings all yee mucky lads and lasses!

Get keen everybody, we have an incredible opportunity for you this winter, we will be travelling to the North Coast of NSW with the boat for 2 weeks to explore some of the best temperate/sub-tropical diving in the world!

We will start at Forster, diving Wolf Rock, then head to South West Rocks for the famous Fish Rock Cave! We will then head north to Coffs Harbour followed by Wooli, to explore the diving of the South and North Solitary Islands!! This will be Week A, Week B will begin at Wooli and then head back down south in the reverse order to improve accessibility for those who cannot afford to come on the whole 2 week adventure!

To secure your spot on this incredible scuba diving adventure, we kindly request a deposit of $500 for each week (There will be no refunds for this unforunately). This will allow us to book the accommodation and pay for fuel as we estimate it will take approx. 2000L to tow the boat there and back. This is the ticketing part of this event. Once we know the final cost of the accommodation, fuel for car and boat, we will create a trip fee on the TeamAPP store for the remaining payment after the trip. 

The total trip cost will cover dinners, accommodation, fuel and boat diving. However, please note that expenses related to gear, snacks, breakfast and lunch are not included in the trip cost. We recommend bringing extra cash to cover these personal expenses during the trip. We will likely co-ordinate lunches and breakfasts to help reduce cost, but these will be optional. 

Please also mention your level of qualification and number of dives :)

Stay tuned for further updates and details regarding the trip. If you have any questions or need more information, feel free to reach out to our team. We’re here to ensure you have a fantastic diving experience!

So please if you’re interested. Put that deposit in :) or if you have questions email me at crahebbard@hotmail.com or msg me on whatsapp, Facebook or SMS.

We will be taking the cruiser and hopefully at least 1-2 other cars, so anyone capable of driving please let me know. We can also meet people who wish to fly to Coffs Harbour airport or into Sydney Airport

event_name: SDI Advanced Adventure Course with Liam
description:
SDI Advanced Adventure Diver
If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge but would like to see what is out there first, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives you can opt for include:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Altitude Diver
Boat Diver
Computer Nitrox Diver
Drift Diver
Marine Ecosystem Awareness
Many more!
This course is intended to introduce you to each of the five specialties selected; not complete comprehension of the chosen specialty. One dive from each of the chosen specialties may apply towards the completion of a specialty certification.

Instructor Liam

event_name: SDI Advanced Adventure Diver COURSE  with Alex
description:
SDI Advanced Adventure Diver
If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge but would like to see what is out there first, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives you can opt for include:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Altitude Diver
Boat Diver
Computer Nitrox Diver
Drift Diver
Marine Ecosystem Awareness
Many more!
This course is intended to introduce you to each of the five specialties selected; not complete comprehension of the chosen specialty. One dive from each of the chosen specialties may apply towards the completion of a specialty certification.

Instructor Alex

SDI Deep Diver Course
SDI’s Deep Diver Course is a must for any diver wishing to explore a little more, go a little deeper, and learn the risks and benefits associated with deep diving. Many interesting sights lay just beyond open water diver training limits such as: beautiful wall dives, shipwrecks, and critters that don’t come into the shallows.The purpose of this course is to provide the necessary training to plan and execute dives that are beyond the depth range experienced during an SDI Open Water Scuba Diver course, specifically beyond 18 metres/60 feet and to a maximum depth of not greater than 40 metres/130 feet. By the end of the course your knowledge, awareness, and comfort level with deep diving activities will have increased.

Instructor Minh

SDI Wreck Diver
Wreck diving can be one of the most exciting aspects of sport diving, however every effort must be made to maximize safe diving techniques. The SDI Wreck Diver Course will discuss the equipment and techniques commonly employed while wreck diving. This course may be taught as a non-penetration, 2 dives required, or as a limited-penetration course, requiring 3 dives. Limited-penetration is defined as a swim through or within the ambient light of entry point.

Instructor Minh

description:
——
*Trip Info*
——
Omg it's 2025 already?!

What better way to start the new year than being immersed off the peninsula's finest north-facing pier. TB and Sean are leading this OW dive at Rye, followed with a bbq at the shed for dinner whenever we've finished washing our gear.

Unlike the Thursday bbq, this bbq is a bring-your-own (BYO) food and drinks; it is open to people who joined in the dive AND club members who would simply like to join in for a social dinner. Essentially, bring your favourite bbq items in the morning and we'll store in the fridge—incliding bread and special items— the grill master/s will cook and you can eat. If you would like to hang out without eating, that's fine too. TB will bring onions for bbqing.

Whilst this event is set to 9 divers due to current space in club cruiser + one more car, if someone wanting to dive has a car suitable for 5 passengers then please contact TB.

*Please note that the event start time is the meeting time at the shed unless stated otherwise in the briefing*

——
*Dive Sites / Itinerary*
——
It's all Rye bby. Meeting at the shed for 7am, probs leave by 7:30. We'll do maximum of 2 dives. Bring lunch or buy lunch...plenty of options nearby. You will probs need sunscreen and a hat for the surface interval, including water for hydration.

Side note: for your own safety, please don't join to dive if you know you'll be hungover from NYE celebrations. Nothing against partying, but personal safety is essential. Of course you're welcome to join us later for the bbq.

event_name: Rescue course - Take your skills to the next level
description:
Why is the Rescue Diver Course hailed as the ultimate diving challenge? Because it pushes you to become a better, more confident diver while teaching you how to prevent and manage problems in the water.

What makes this course so rewarding?
💪 You’ll gain the skills to not only handle your own safety but also assist others in need.
🤝 You'll learn to be a better dive buddy by practicing problem-solving techniques until they become second nature.
✨ The confidence you build will transform the way you dive, making every underwater adventure safer and more enjoyable.

If you're ready to take your skills to the next level and experience the pride that comes with helping others, this course is for you.

Join us for this unforgettable journey! 🌊

Why Take This Course?
✅ Learn essential rescue techniques
✅ Improve problem-solving skills underwater
✅ Boost your confidence as a diver
✅ Prepare for advanced diving certifications

Course Highlights:
🔹 Self-rescue and stress management
🔹 Recognizing and responding to diver emergencies
🔹 Rescue scenarios and techniques
🔹 Real-life simulations

Prerequisites: Adventure Diver/Junior Adventure Diver (or qualifying certification) with completed Underwater Navigation Dive 

Provide proof of current First Response Adult and Child Emergency Care Provider and
Oxygen Administration Provider (where local law permits) certification or equivalent

What’s Included:
🌟 Expert instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Let me know if you are interested so we can organise the prerequisite 

Info.scubabui@gmail.com

Trip Info

Jeff, like you and me, is a passionate diver who lives and breathes the underwater world. His journey began with the MUUC, where he took his first plunge, and he’s never looked back since. A beloved member of the club, Jeff’s enthusiasm and warm spirit make every dive an unforgettable experience. Whether it’s sharing stories, offering guidance, or simply spreading his infectious joy, he embodies the true spirit of the diving community.

It’s a charter boat diving trip, and we will head to J4, one of Melbourne’s iconic wreck dive sites. J4 is a J-Class submarine that was intentionally scuttled in 1927 and now rests at a depth of 26-38 meters. This site offers an exciting wreck diving experience with a well-preserved submarine structure, home to diverse marine life such as schools of fish, sea stars, and nudibranchs.

event_name: Gooddivers- TDI Advanced Nitrox course & TDI Decompression Procedures course (ANDP)
description:
TDI Advanced Nitrox course & TDI Decompression Procedures course (ANDP)
Course date 18,19,22,23rd Feb


Combine your TDI Advanced Nitrox course with the TDI Decompression Procedures course. This will prepare you for planned staged decompression diving and is your first step beyond the normal recreational limits.

The TDI Advanced Nitrox Course qualifies divers to use enriched air nitrox from EAN 21 through EAN 100 percent within your current certification level to a maximum depth of 40 metres/130 feet during dives that do not require staged decompression.

The TDI Decompression Procedures Course prepares you for planned staged decompression diving. With a maximum operating depth of 45 metres/150 feet, this course is your first step beyond the normal sport diving limits. The TDI Decompression Procedures Course combined with the TDI Advanced Nitrox course form the foundation of all other technical courses. After these two courses and some additional experience, the stage has been set for you to move onto additional technical levels.

Course prerequisites:

Minimum age 18

Minimum certification of TDI nitrox diver,PADI enriched diver or equivalent

Proof of 25 logged open water dives

Course outline (subject to change depends on the weather or other reasons)

Orientation date will commence near starting period

any questions to Info.gooddivers@gmail.com

description:
SDI Advanced Adventure Diver
If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge but would like to see what is out there first, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives you can opt for include:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Altitude Diver
Boat Diver
Computer Nitrox Diver
Drift Diver
Marine Ecosystem Awareness
Many more!
This course is intended to introduce you to each of the five specialties selected; not complete comprehension of the chosen specialty. One dive from each of the chosen specialties may apply towards the completion of a specialty certification.

SDI Advanced Adventure Diver
If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge but would like to see what is out there first, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives you can opt for include:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Altitude Diver
Boat Diver
Computer Nitrox Diver
Drift Diver
Marine Ecosystem Awareness
Many more!
This course is intended to introduce you to each of the five specialties selected; not complete comprehension of the chosen specialty. One dive from each of the chosen specialties may apply towards the completion of a specialty certification.

Flemming

event_name: Rescue course with Flemming 29th to 30th March
description:
Why is the Rescue Diver Course hailed as the ultimate diving challenge? Because it pushes you to become a better, more confident diver while teaching you how to prevent and manage problems in the water.

What makes this course so rewarding?
💪 You’ll gain the skills to not only handle your own safety but also assist others in need.
🤝 You’ll learn to be a better dive buddy by practicing problem-solving techniques until they become second nature.
✨ The confidence you build will transform the way you dive, making every underwater adventure safer and more enjoyable.

If you’re ready to take your skills to the next level and experience the pride that comes with helping others, this course is for you.

Join us for this unforgettable journey! 🌊

Why Take This Course?
✅ Learn essential rescue techniques
✅ Improve problem-solving skills underwater
✅ Boost your confidence as a diver
✅ Prepare for advanced diving certifications

Course Highlights:
🔹 Self-rescue and stress management
🔹 Recognizing and responding to diver emergencies
🔹 Rescue scenarios and techniques
🔹 Real-life simulations

Prerequisites: Adventure Diver/Junior Adventure Diver (or qualifying certification) with completed Underwater Navigation Dive

Provide proof of current First Response Adult and Child Emergency Care Provider and
Oxygen Administration Provider (where local law permits) certification or equivalent

What’s Included:
🌟 Expert instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Trip Info
Meeting at shed Saturday 12th April 04:00 AM

Join us for a Wilsons Prom trip – This will be the perfect opportunity to get in some dives in a beautiful area maybe catch some crays, and share in some wonderful food including handmade pizza!
Meeting in the shed at 04:00 on Saturday, April 12th and returning late on the 14th, we plan to do 3 consecutive days of boat diving!

Bring lunch/snacks for the first day of diving – We will make dinner together on Saturday and meals together for the rest of the trip. Kit will bring his Pizza Oven and fresh dough with some toppings. BYO Alcohol and soft drinks. 
Please text Kit if you have any dietary requirements.

The Landcruiser plus a couple of cars will be leaving from the shed and returning to the shed after the trip and will have enough room for everyone and their gear.
If anyone wishes to drive separately/directly there, let me know.

We will stay at an Airbnb conveniently located in Port Welshpool.
The small club compressor is coming along for the trip, to allow for air fills at the Airbnb.

To secure your spot please purchase a ticket on the event!

The deposit payment covers accommodation, after the trip there will be a separate payment for the shared expenses for cruiser and boat usage, air fills, and shared food and snacks.

Don’t forget to renew your fishing license if you plan to catch a cray!

description:
——
Trip Info
——
Just a couple of chill shore dives. Most likely Blairgowrie and then Point Linley but still in the process of being researched. Aiming to be a nice gentle refresher for some people who haven’t dived in a while, ideally featuring an octopus if we’re lucky. Depth shouldn’t exceed 10m at any of the sites (and except for a few spots we’ll be lucky to get deeper than 5m). 

Meeting time TBC but will be on the earlier side (7am) since we need to be finished up by about 4:30pm.

event_name: [Boat + Shore][OW] Lesbian Day of Ocean Vis Dive
description:
Hey Scuba Huns 👋

Tomorrow is Lesbian Day of Visibility, which means if you’re a lesbian, a bit lesbian, or with a lesbian you’ll have great ocean visibility! 

I’m planning a charter dive with Scubabo leaving Portsea Pier at 9:00 to check out Dragon’s Lair (10-18m) just outside the heads. Around a 30min boat ride to and from with around 40mins of dive time. 

Then lunch. 

Then Linley Point (4-7m) on the way back. 
Linley point does require some light bouldering while in full scuba gear for entry, then a beach exit. 

Other Important Notes:

1. I’ve arranged for a lesbian discount from Scubabo - so it’s $78.75 for the charter dive, however you’ll have to transfer me and I’ll book the dive as a group purchase.

2. This is a female priority dive in the spirit of the day. After 20:00 today the dive will then open to non-female divers to fill leftover spots.
3. Pack gear tonight at 18:00 before karaoke for an easy depart tomorrow morning. Remember, the boat doesn’t wait!
4. I do have some other plans in the evening so I don’t want to drag any part of the schedule significantly. 
5. There is abalone at Dragons so buy a 3-day fishing license for $10 and bring a catch bag if interested. Fishing restrictions stipulate only 5x abalone per person and must be over 11cm in size. Use a measuring tool if you are unsure. 

Trip Details

Leader Phone: ‪+61 410 314 892‬

White Rock is a compelling new documentary helmed by Damon Gameau (2040, That Sugar Film) that brings together scientists, chefs, fishers, and policymakers to tackle one of the biggest environmental challenges facing our oceans - the explosion of longspined sea urchins devastating Australia’s kelp forests.

A ticketed event at the Ian Potter Auditorium, University of Melbourne. Tickets on sale here:
https://events.humanitix.com/white-rock-film-and-community-q-and-a-melbourne-victorian-premiere

Start Time: 5.30pm (doors open for a 6.00pm start)

End Time: 8.00pm

Venue: Ian Potter Auditorium, Kenneth Myer Building, 30 Royal Parade, Parkville 3010

Tickets are General: $20 | UoM $10

UoM staff, students and alumni get 50% off with this code: GSRUNIMELB


Posting this to allow fellow interested people to gather and go together. All are welcome to attend.

Join us for our Dive Trip Organiser Workshop and discover everything you need to know about becoming a future trip leader for our club! Whether you're a seasoned diver ready to step into a leadership role or simply curious about what it takes to guide a dive trip, this workshop is designed for all club members eager to learn and grow.

I will be hosting this workshop on the 8th of Mayl at 5:30pm at social space B (at the back of Nona Lee sports center, near the running track) -- if you can't find it, head to the shed and shoot us a message, someone will come grab you! 

What You'll Learn:
Pre-Dive Prep: How to check weather conditions, organize gear, and ensure everyone is ready for an epic dive day.
Dive Leadership: Learn how to brief divers, manage groups, and prioritize safety.
Emergency Protocols: What to do in emergency situations and who to contact.
Post-Dive Wrap-Up: Shed cleanup, gear maintenance, and keeping things tidy for the next adventure.

Why Join?
Skill Development: Hone your organizational and leadership skills.
Rewarding Experience: Organise trips, make new friends, and help others explore the underwater world.

💡 Fun Fact: Trip Organisers are volunteers, not professionals—so the focus is on having fun and creating memorable dives for everyone!

Don't miss this chance to become an essential part of our diving community. Click below to RSVP or ask any questions!

Xiang

Hi all boat Trainees and interested parties! 

Michael and I will be running a dry boat workshop at the club starting 6:30pm on the Wednesday 28th May. 

The purpose of this session is to introduce you to running boat trips with the club boat and learn various features of the vessel on land.

Session duration around 2h. 

A future session will take you on the water for practical exercises.

Dear Divers! 

Gear workshop returns! In this edition we will be talking and getting started on building a dedicated diving toolkit.

Most tools can be sourced from hardware stores or online and some are more custom.

Come and learn more!

event_name: SDI Drysuit Diver Course w/ Flemming
description:
Please note: The date is flexible; get in touch if you are interested in the course.
Cost w/ 2 students, 290$ with 3 or more students 250$. 
Questions: Training@Divesport.com.au or whatsapp 0429 779 607

The SDI Dry Suit Diver Course will open up a whole new world of diving to you. Many of the diving environments around the world are better experienced in a dry suit. Why? These areas have an abundance of marine life waiting to be seen and photographed – they just happen to be a little colder! Dry suits are also used in tropical water during the cooler months.This course allows the student to develop the knowledge and skills to properly use a dry suit. It will discuss the types of dry suits, accessories, maintenance and how to make basic repairs.

event_name: [AOW + OW] Big Ol’ July Scuba Trip: Kilsby Sinkhole + Whyalla + Rapid Bay Jetty
description:
Trip Info
——
Let’s go see the spectacular mating rituals of the Giant Cuttlefish in Whyalla Bay! These camouflaging cephalopods perform dances, battle one another, and showcase their colour/texture/pattern shifting abilities using their chromatophores (cells containing pigment sacs) - all in the name of love (aka insemination). 

+On the way to and from Whyalla we’ll have a few side adventures.

IMPORTANT NOTE: We are keeping an eye on the SA Karenia Mikimotoi algal bloom situation that is affecting the area and sea life. The latest update is that the bloom has shifted from areas by Whyalla to Coorong. To mitigate this we are trying to make bookings with late full refund policies around June 30. 

——

Stay the night at Blue Lake Holiday Park.

2nd of July: ADELAIDE: 7:30 departure for a 5hr drive. Casual hangout in Adelaide. Possible hike, gallery visit, or night dive. Stay the night in Adelaide.

3rd & 4th of July: WHYALLA: Exact dive spots not yet selected. 

5th of July: RAPID BAY JETTY: Morning departure from Whyalla for 5.5hr drive to Rapid Bay Jetty for a dive and look for the leafy sea dragon. Stay the night around Coorong National Park. 

6th of July: MELBOURNE: 6hr drive back to Melbourne.

event_name: ex MUUC Singaporean Marine Biologist researcher showcases Life of Seagrass and Marine life
description:
Ever wondered what it’s like to do marine science in Singapore — or what secrets lie hidden in the swaying meadows of seagrass?

Join us for a special evening with a guest speaker who’s swapped tropical waters for Victoria’s cooler coastlines. With a background in marine ecology and a life lived between science and scuba, they’ll share stories from working on seagrass research in Southeast Asia — and what it's like doing fieldwork (and diving) in one of the world’s most unexpected marine cities.

🗓 Date: 10th July
🕕 Time: 6.00pm
📍 Location: Medley Theatre (163) Redmond Barry (across from the Dive Club)

MUUC divers, science lovers, and curious minds all welcome. Come along — you might just see our local waters in a whole new way. 😉

Dive Adventure Notice: Philippine Siren Liveaboard — Tubbataha Reef 2026
Announcing a premium scuba adventure to the Philippines!

Trip Highlights
Dates: Depart 9 June 2026 | Return 17 June 2026

Destination: Tubbataha Reef — UNESCO World Heritage Site & Marine Protected Area

Vessel: 40m "Philippine Siren" liveaboard, max 16 guests, ensuite cabins, fully equipped for underwater photography

What’s Included
Return flights ex. Melbourne to Puerto Princesa (via Manila)

All transfers, taxes, and airline fees (subject to change until ticketed)

1 night twin-share in Manila Airport (Belmont Hotel)

6 nights in twin/double-share cabin aboard the Philippine Siren

Up to 18 dives (including up to 4 dives per diving day) with all dive support

All meals on board; breakfast in hotels

Unlimited water, tea, coffee, soft drinks on board

Why Choose This Trip?
Technical diver friendly: Ample support for experienced/technical divers

Advanced diving destination with strong currents and deep drop-offs

See hammerhead sharks, whale sharks, manta rays, turtles, vibrant corals and more

Spacious dive deck and dedicated camera areas for professionals

Dive Requirements
PADI Advanced Open Water (or equivalent)

Minimum 30 logged dives and experience in strong currents

Extras & Extension
Tubbataha Marine Park fees: USD $120 per person (subject to change)

Not included: travel insurance, personal expenses, excess baggage

Possible trip extension: Consider adding time for extra adventures post-liveaboard — ask for details!

How to Book
Complete online booking form and send with $1,500 deposit per person

Partial payments/instalments required

All details on the Dive Adventures website

If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge but would like to see what is out there first, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education.

What you can expect to learn:
An overview of the SDI Deep Diver and SDI Navigation Diver specialties
Basic insight to three other chosen specialties of your choice as an introduction to that specific area in diving
Some of the in-water skills include:

Successfully perform skills listed for dive one of the three chosen specialties as well as both core specialties
Deep Diver open water dive
Test and check all equipment
Familiarisation with dive site
Descend to planned depth, do not exceed any pre-planned limits
Dive according to plan at a depth limited to 30 metres/ 100 feet
Divers between the age of 10 and 15 cannot exceed 21 metres/70 feet
Ascend to safety stop
Exit and log dive
Navigation open water dive
Establish a reference point, plan dive, and enter water from shore/boat
Practice out and back technique on surface
Squares and triangles on surface
Perform a square on the bottom
Perform a triangle on the bottom
Ascend to safety stop

Meeting in the shed at 04:00 on Sunday, November 16th and returning late on the 18th, we plan to do 3 consecutive days of boat diving!

event_name: SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course w/ Flemming
description:
Course Fee: A$520
When signing up, please send Flemming a WhatsApp message on 0429 779 607

SDI Advanced Adventure Diver certifies you to dive up to 30 meters, opening up a wide range of boat diving in Victoria! 

If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge and skills, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives we will do are:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Night Diving
Wreck Diving

You will also have the option of doing your Nitrox certification as an add-on to the course for 95$, please contact Flemming if this is something you are interested in.

The course price includes 1x boat fee but does not include transport and gear rental for the 5 dives.

Any questions, please feel free to contact Flemming at 
training@divesport.com.au or WhatsApp +61 429 779 607

Trip Info
Meeting at shed Thursday 18th November 04:00 AM

With Christmas just around the corner,  there's no better time to get a hand caught cray to wow your friends and family!

Join us for a Wilsons Prom trip – This will be the perfect opportunity to get in some dives in a beautiful area maybe catch some crays, and share in some wonderful food including handmade pizza!
Meeting in the shed at 04:00 on Thursday, December 18th and returning late on the 21st, we plan to do 4 consecutive days of boat diving!

Bring lunch/snacks for the first day of diving – We will make dinner and meals together for the rest of the trip. Kit will bring his Pizza Oven and fresh dough with some toppings. BYO Alcohol and soft drinks. 
Please text Kit if you have any dietary requirements.

The Landcruiser plus a couple of cars will be leaving from the shed and returning to the shed after the trip and will have enough room for everyone and their gear.
If anyone wishes to drive separately/directly there, let me know.

We will stay at an Airbnb conveniently located in Port Welshpool.
The small club compressor is coming along for the trip, to allow for air fills at the Airbnb.

To secure your spot please purchase a ticket on the event!

The deposit payment covers accommodation, after the trip there will be a separate payment for the shared expenses for cruiser and boat usage, air fills, and shared food and snacks.

Don’t forget to renew your fishing license if you plan to catch a cray!

Are you ready to take the plunge and explore the incredible world beneath the waves? Join our Open Water Diver Course and unlock the gateway to breathtaking underwater adventures.

What You'll Learn:
✅ Essential diving skills
✅ Safety procedures
✅ Equipment use and maintenance
✅ Experience open water dives

Who Can Join?
This course is perfect for beginners and anyone looking to start their scuba diving journey. No prior experience required – just a passion for adventure!

You must be able to swim before starting the course.

What’s Included:
🌟 Professional instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Sign Up Today!
Spaces are limited, so don’t miss your chance to explore the underwater world.

To register:
1. Read our training policy at: muuc.org.au/courses/training-policy
2. Create an account on Teamapp including your first and last name 
3. Purchase ticket in this event to secure your spot
4. RSVP yes to the event
5. Make sure to track notifications on this event for official course comms.

Prior to the course:
1. Make sure to go to the teamapp store and do the Liability Waiver, Gear Deposit and purchase the applicable membership category to be eligible to receive the e-learning that must be completed prior to starting the practical component of the course. Please complete ASAP

2. Complete your e-learning after receiving the code from your instructor, the e-learning must be completed at least 1 week prior to the course starting. 

3. Make sure to complete your course orientation as per the request of your instructor

Course inquiries:
Use comments section here or email us:
MUUC Course Coordinator 
courses@muuc.org.au

event_name: Car insurance Renewal is on Feb 18th
Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.
email: info.scubabui@gmail.com

event_name: SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course (30M)  w/ Flemming
description:
Course Fee: A$580 (2 boat dives included!)
To lock in your spot please grab a ticket and send Flemming a WhatsApp message on 0429 779 607

SDI Advanced Adventure Diver certifies you to dive up to 30 meters, opening up a wide range of boat diving in Victoria! 

If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge and skills, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives we will do are:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Night Diving
Wreck Diving

You will also have the option of doing your Nitrox certification as an add-on to the course for 145$, please contact Flemming if this is something you are interested in.

The course fee includes 2x charterboat dives but does not include transport and gear rental for the 5 dives.

Students must have full scuba gear, including diving computer, SMB, compass and two dive lights.

If suitable club gear is not available, I can help you access special pricing on rentals/purchases of dive gear through local dive stores.

There will be an opt-in option to stay at an Airbnb nearer the dive sites during the course.

Any questions, please feel free to contact Flemming at 
training@divesport.com.au or WhatsApp +61 429 779 607

event_name: 🌊 [Thursday 19th Feb update] Dive Trip Organiser Workshop 🌊
description:
Join us for our Dive Trip Organiser Workshop and discover everything you need to know about becoming a future trip organiser for our club! Whether you're a seasoned diver ready to step into a leadership role or simply curious about what it takes to guide a dive trip, this workshop is designed for all club members eager to learn and grow.

I will be hosting this workshop on the 19th of Feb at 5:30pm at social space B (at the back of Nona Lee sports centre, near the running track) -- if you can't find it, head to the shed and shoot us a message, someone will come grab you! 

What You'll Learn:
Pre-Dive Prep: How to check weather conditions, organise gear, and ensure everyone is ready for an epic dive day.
Dive Leadership: Learn how to brief divers, manage groups, and prioritise safety.
Emergency Protocols: What to do in emergency situations and who to contact.
Post-Dive Wrap-Up: Shed cleanup, gear maintenance and keeping things tidy for the next adventure.

Why Join?
Skill Development: Hone your organisational and leadership skills.
Rewarding Experience: Organise trips, make new friends and help others explore the underwater world.

💡 Fun Fact: Trip Organisers are volunteers, not professionals—so the focus is on having fun and creating memorable dives for everyone!

Don't miss this chance to become an essential part of our diving community. Click below to RSVP or ask any questions!

A long, relaxed shallow dive at Rye Pier — one of Port Phillip Bay’s most reliable macro sites. The pylons are thick with marine growth and attract nudibranchs, seahorses, pipefish, spider crabs, rays and the occasional octopus tucked into the sand. The gentle slope and 6–8m depth allow for extended bottom time and slow, controlled exploration.

Currently 5 but can extend to more people if they have a car.

Come and join us for our MUUC dive-themed trivia night!

Whether you're an aspiring Open Water student, a seasoned tech-bro or just someone who loves the water and the creatures that live within it, this event will test your knowledge on marine life, famous dive sites, dive history, popculture and everything else you might expect to find beneath the surface. 

Bring along your dive buddies for a night of fun, snacks and a chance to win some fun prizes (or the glory of being crowned MUUC dive trivia champions).

We will meet up at the dive shed and make our way to the sports centre after dive planning at 7:45 pm :)

Details below:

event_name: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.

event_name: SDI Open Water Course (Instructor Minh) March 13-15th [6 Sets]
description:
Are you ready to take the plunge and explore the incredible world beneath the waves? Join our Open Water Diver Course and unlock the gateway to breathtaking underwater adventures.

What You'll Learn:
✅ Essential diving skills
✅ Safety procedures
✅ Equipment use and maintenance
✅ Experience open water dives

Who Can Join?
This course is perfect for beginners and anyone looking to start their scuba diving journey. No prior experience required – just a passion for adventure!

You must be able to swim before starting the course.

What’s Included:
🌟 Professional instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Itinerary:
13th March: Pool Day 5pm-9pm
14th March: Ocean Day 1 ALL DAY
15th March: Ocean Day 2 Signoff Day ALL DAY

Sign Up Today!
Spaces are limited, so don’t miss your chance to explore the underwater world.

To register:
1. Read our training policy at: muuc.org.au/courses/training-policy
2. Create an account on Teamapp including your first and last name 
3. Purchase ticket in this event to secure your spot
4. RSVP yes to the event
5. Make sure to track notifications on this event for official course comms.

Prior to the course:
1. Make sure to go to the teamapp store and do the Liability Waiver, Gear Deposit and purchase the applicable membership category to be eligible to receive the e-learning that must be completed prior to starting the practical component of the course. Please complete ASAP

2. Complete your e-learning after receiving the code from your instructor, the e-learning must be completed at least 1 week prior to the course starting. 

3. Make sure to complete your course orientation as per the request of your instructor

Course inquiries:
Use comments section here or email us:
MUUC Course Coordinator:
courses@muuc.org.au
SDI Instructor Minh:
info.scubabui@gmail.com

description:

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

email: info.scubabui@gmail.com

Interested in improving your underwater photography? Whether you’re new to shooting underwater or looking to refine your technique, this workshop will walk you through the fundamentals of creating compelling underwater images.

Join us for an evening session where we’ll cover the essential elements of underwater photography, including how to set up your camera equipment, understanding the exposure square (ISO, aperture, shutter speed, and strobe power), and how lighting and strobe positioning affect your images underwater. We’ll also explore composition techniques and discuss how to move beyond technical shots to create images that tell a story.

Topics will include:
	•	Underwater camera and housing setup
	•	Understanding the exposure square
	•	Strobe position 
	•	Composition techniques underwater
	•	Turning photos into visual stories

The session will also include example photos and practical tips drawn from real diving situations, competitions, and field experience.

Whether you dive with a compact camera, mirrorless system, or DSLR, you’ll walk away with practical techniques you can apply on your next dive.

📍 Location: Social A
🕢 Time: 7:30 PM – 9:30 PM
📅 Date: March 19

Come curious, bring your questions, and let’s talk about how to capture the underwater world more effectively.

description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

event_name: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.
email: info.scubabui@gmail.com

event_name: [postponed] SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course (30M)  w/ Flemming
description:
Course Fee: A$580 (2 boat dives included!)
To lock in your spot please grab a ticket to lock in your spot and send Flemming a WhatsApp message on 0429 779 607

SDI Advanced Adventure Diver certifies you to dive up to 30 meters, opening up a wide range of boat diving in Victoria! 

If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge and skills, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives we will do are:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Night Diving
Wreck Diving

You will also have the option of doing your Nitrox certification as an add-on to the course for 150$, please contact Flemming if this is something you are interested in.

The course fee includes 2x boat dives but does not include transport, airfills and gear rental for the 5 dives.

Students must have full scuba gear, including diving computer, SMB, compass and two dive lights.

If suitable club gear is not available, I can help you access special pricing on rentals/purchases of dive gear through local dive stores.

There will be an opt-in option for the group to stay at an Airbnb closer to the dive sites during the course.

Any questions, please feel free to contact Flemming at 
training@divesport.com.au or WhatsApp +61 429 779 607

event_name: Open water course with Minh 17th 18th 19th April
description:
Are you ready to take the plunge and explore the incredible world beneath the waves? Join our Open Water Diver Course and unlock the gateway to breathtaking underwater adventures.

What You'll Learn:
✅ Essential diving skills
✅ Safety procedures
✅ Equipment use and maintenance
✅ Experience open water dives

Who Can Join?
This course is perfect for beginners and anyone looking to start their scuba diving journey. No prior experience required – just a passion for adventure!

You must be able to swim before starting the course.

What’s Included:
🌟 Professional instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Itinerary:
17th April: Pool Day 5pm-9pm
18th April: Ocean Day 1 ALL DAY
19th April: Ocean Day 2 Signoff Day ALL DAY

Sign Up Today!
Spaces are limited, so don’t miss your chance to explore the underwater world.

To register:
1. Read our training policy at: muuc.org.au/courses/training-policy
2. Create an account on Teamapp including your first and last name 
3. Purchase ticket in this event to secure your spot
4. RSVP yes to the event
5. Make sure to track notifications on this event for official course comms.

2. Complete your e-learning after receiving the code from your instructor, the e-learning must be completed at least 1 week prior to the course starting. 

3. Make sure to complete your course orientation as per the request of your instructor

Course inquiries:
Use comments section here or email us:
MUUC Course Coordinator:
courses@muuc.org.au
SDI Instructor Minh:
info.scubabui@gmail.com
